content.problemStatement: Given a chain of matrices A1,..., An denoted by an array of size n+1, find out the minimum number of operations to multiply these n matrices .
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer array of size n+1, where n is the number of matrices. Each element in the array represents the number of rows in the corresponding matrix.
content.outputFormat: The minimum number of scalar multiplications needed to compute the product of the given matrices.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using dynamic programming by considering all possible partitions of the matrix chain and choosing the one that results in the minimum number of scalar multiplications.

code.Python3:
class Solution:
    # Function to find the minimum multiplication cost
    def matrixChainOrder(self, arr, i, j):
        # Base case: if only one matrix is present, no multiplication needed
        if i == j:
            return 0

        # Initialize minimum cost as very large
        min_cost = float("inf")

        # Try all possible partitions
        for k in range(i, j):
            # Cost of multiplying matrices from i to k
            cost1 = self.matrixChainOrder(arr, i, k)

            # Cost of multiplying matrices from k+1 to j
            cost2 = self.matrixChainOrder(arr, k + 1, j)

            # Cost of multiplying the two resulting matrices
            costMultiply = arr[i - 1] * arr[k] * arr[j]

            # Total cost for this partition
            total = cost1 + cost2 + costMultiply

            # Update minimum
            min_cost = min(min_cost, total)

        return min_cost


# Driver code
if __name__ == "__main__":
    sol = Solution()
    arr = [40, 20, 30, 10, 30]
    n = len(arr)
    print("Minimum number of multiplications is:",
          sol.matrixChainOrder(arr, 1, n - 1))
